description: Resilience hardening: Improve graceful shutdown (drain SSE connections), add connection limits for SSE, and harden the server against event-night failure modes (too many SSE clients, stale connections, memory pressure).
steps: In server/kernel/bus.js, add a MAX_CLIENTS constant (default 200 - more than enough for even a large crew). When a new SSE client tries to attach and the limit is reached, respond with 503 and a clear message. This prevents memory exhaustion from leaked connections.

In server/kernel/bus.js attach(), add a per-person connection limit (max 3 concurrent SSE connections per personId). If exceeded, drop the oldest connection for that person before attaching the new one. This handles the common case of a crew member refreshing their browser tab repeatedly.

In server/main.js shutdown handler, improve graceful shutdown: (1) stop accepting new connections immediately, (2) close all SSE connections cleanly with a 'shutdown' event so clients know to reconnect, (3) wait up to 3 seconds for in-flight requests to complete, (4) then snapshot and exit. Replace the current simplistic approach.

In server/kernel/bus.js, add a stale connection reaper: every 60 seconds, check all SSE clients. If a client's underlying socket is destroyed or has not had a successful write in 90 seconds (beyond the 25s ping interval), drop it. This cleans up zombie connections from crashed devices.
Add test case to smoke.mjs: connect 5 SSE streams as the same user, verify only 3 remain active (oldest 2 are dropped). Verify the /api/health endpoint reports the correct online count.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes including new SSE connection limit tests
Server gracefully shuts down SSE connections on SIGTERM instead of abruptly killing them
Max 3 concurrent SSE connections per user are enforced
Global SSE client cap of 200 is enforced with 503 response
verification: Run npm test and confirm all checks pass
